title: Hundred Islands National Park
shortDesc: 124 islands and islets scattered across the crystal-clear Lingayen Gulf.
description:
Hundred Islands National Park is one of the Philippines' most treasured natural wonders, located in Alaminos City in Pangasinan. Spanning approximately 1,844 hectares of land and sea, the park is home to 124 stunning islands and islets — only three of which are developed for tourism: Governor's Island, Children's Island, and Quezon Island.

Declared a National Park in 1940, Hundred Islands offers a rich tapestry of marine biodiversity, limestone formations, white sand beaches, and pristine turquoise waters. Whether you're a nature enthusiast, an adventure seeker, or simply in search of relaxation, the park delivers an unforgettable experience that few destinations in the country can match.
tips: Best visited during the dry season (November to May) for calm seas and sunny skies.
Bring reef-safe sunscreen to protect your skin and the park's marine ecosystems.
Wear water shoes — some island rocks can be sharp underfoot.
Hire a licensed local boatman at the Alaminos City Pier for the best island tour experience.

title: Bolinao Falls
shortDesc: Stunning cascading waterfalls perfect for swimming in cool refreshing waters.
description:
Bolinao Falls offers a refreshing escape with its clear, cool waters and beautiful cascading falls surrounded by lush greenery. Located in the municipality of Bolinao in Pangasinan, these falls are a favorite spot for tourists seeking a natural swimming pool away from the heat.

There are multiple falls in the area, each with its own character and charm. The waters are crystal clear and cool, making it a perfect spot for a refreshing dip after a long drive.
tips: Visit on weekdays to avoid weekend crowds.
Bring water shoes — the rocks near the falls can be slippery.
Swimming is allowed in designated safe areas only.

title: Enchanted Cave
shortDesc: Underground pool with crystal clear waters hidden deep inside a natural cave.
description: Enchanted Cave is one of the must-visit attractions in Bolinao, Pangasinan. It is a massive cave made of coral rocks and is believed to have been underwater thousands of years ago. Inside the cave is a clear natural pool where tourists can swim in the cool, refreshing underground waters.
tips: Wear a swimsuit — you will get wet inside the cave pool.
Life vests are provided and required for non-swimmers.
Keep noise to a minimum to preserve the cave's natural atmosphere.

title: Cape Bolinao Lighthouse
shortDesc: Historic lighthouse offering breathtaking panoramic views of the West Philippine Sea.
description: The Cape Bolinao Lighthouse is one of the tallest lighthouses in the Philippines, standing at approximately 32 meters. Built in 1903 by American, British, and Filipino engineers, it was originally designed to guide ships navigating the treacherous waters of the West Philippine Sea.
tips: Visit during sunset for the most spectacular views.
Wear comfortable shoes — the climb involves steep stairs.

title: Lingayen Gulf Beach
shortDesc: Historic gulf known for its long gray sand beaches and rich wartime history.
description: Lingayen Gulf is renowned not only for its extensive, beautiful gray sand beach but also for its historical significance as the landing site of General Douglas MacArthur's allied forces during World War II on January 9, 1945.
tips: Visit early morning for a peaceful sunrise experience.
The boulevard area has many seafood restaurants.

title: Manaoag Church
shortDesc: A major pilgrimage site for Catholics, home to the miraculous Lady of Manaoag.
description: The Minor Basilica of Our Lady of the Most Holy Rosary of Manaoag is one of the most important Catholic pilgrimage sites in the Philippines. Millions of devotees visit the basilica each year.
tips: Dress modestly — shorts and sleeveless tops are not allowed inside.
Visit on weekdays to avoid the massive weekend pilgrim crowds.

title: Patar Beach
shortDesc: Famous golden sand beach renowned for its stunning sunsets and clear blue waters.
description: Often referred to as the 'Boracay of the North', Patar Beach in Bolinao features a long stretch of golden-brown sand facing the West Philippine Sea. The beach is known for its spectacular sunsets.
tips: Arrive before 5PM for the best sunset view.
ATV rentals are available along the beach for exploring the sand dunes.

title: Dagupan Bangus Festival
shortDesc: Annual vibrant celebration of the city's world-famous milkfish and local culture.
description: The Bangus Festival is an annual celebration held every April or May in Dagupan City, honoring the milkfish (bangus) industry that the city is world-renowned for.
tips: Book accommodations months in advance.
Try the iconic boneless bangus.

title: Cabongaoan Beach
shortDesc: Pristine white sand beach featuring the famous 'Death Pool'.
description: Cabongaoan Beach offers a serene coastal retreat in Burgos. It is famous for its white sand and the stunning 'Death Pool', a natural tidal pool where visitors can take a thrilling plunge during low tide.
tips: Be cautious when visiting the Death Pool during high tide.
Bring your own camping gear if you plan to stay overnight.

title: Tambobong Beach
shortDesc: A peaceful fishing village featuring crystal-clear waters and white sand.
description: Tambobong Beach in Dasol is a hidden gem with its uncrowded, pristine white sand shores and clear waters. It's a fantastic place for island-hopping adventures to nearby islets like Colibra Island.
tips: Hire a boat to explore Colibra Island.
Buy fresh seafood directly from the local fishermen.

title: Tondol White Sand Beach
shortDesc: Known for having one of the longest shallow sandbars in the region.
description: Located in the municipality of Anda, Tondol White Sand Beach is famous for its long, shallow sandbar that reveals itself during low tide, allowing visitors to walk to the nearby Tanduyong Island.
tips: Visit during low tide to experience walking to the neighboring island.
It's very safe for children due to the extremely shallow waters.

title: Pangasinan Longganisa
shortDesc: A garlicky and distinctively seasoned local sausage famous in Alaminos.
description: Alaminos Longganisa is a unique Filipino pork sausage identified by its distinct garlic flavor and the unique way it is segmented using grass strands (buli). It's a staple breakfast delicacy served with fried rice and eggs.
tips: Best enjoyed with a splash of local spiced vinegar.
Can be bought fresh from the Alaminos public market.

title: Dagupan Pigar-Pigar
shortDesc: A beloved Dagupan street food made of deep-fried beef and cabbage.
description: Pigar-Pigar is a popular Dagupan delicacy originating from the streets of Galvan. It consists of thin slices of beef or carabeef deep-fried and served with generous portions of cabbage and onions.
tips: Best eaten hot with rice and a dip of soy sauce, calamansi, and chili.
Visit Galvan Street at night for the authentic experience.

title: Puto Calasiao
shortDesc: Bite-sized, sweet, and sticky rice cakes famously made in Calasiao.
description: Puto Calasiao is a traditional Filipino steamed rice cake made from fermented semi-glutinous rice. These bite-sized treats are mildly sweet and wonderfully chewy, often paired with grated coconut or savory dishes like dinuguan.
tips: Buy them fresh in the morning near the Calasiao Church.
Try the various flavors: pandan, ube, and cheese.

title: Pangasinan Tupig
shortDesc: Grilled glutinous rice cakes wrapped in banana leaves.
description: Tupig is a beloved Pangasinan snack made from ground glutinous rice, coconut milk, and muscovado sugar, wrapped in banana leaves and grilled over hot coals. The grilling imparts a fantastic smoky aroma.
tips: Eat it while it's hot for the best texture.
Easily found being sold by vendors along the MacArthur Highway.

title: Dagupan Bangus
shortDesc: The world-famous, sweet, and tasty milkfish of Dagupan.
description: Dagupan Bangus is celebrated globally for its sweet taste and lack of a muddy flavor, a result of the city's pristine brackish waters. It can be prepared in various ways: grilled, daing (marinated), or sinigang.
tips: Look for the authentic Dagupan Bangus seal when buying.
Best enjoyed boneless and marinated (daing).

title: Lingayen Bagoong Festival
shortDesc: A festive celebration of Lingayen's prominent bagoong (shrimp paste) industry.
description: The Bagoong Festival honors the thriving bagoong industry in Lingayen, Pangasinan. It features colorful parades, street dances, and culinary competitions utilizing the famous condiment that gives many Filipino dishes their distinct umami flavor.
tips: Don't leave without buying bottles of authentic Lingayen bagoong.
Watch out for the creative culinary uses of bagoong during the cook-offs.

title: Pista'y Dayat Festival
shortDesc: The 'Feast of the Sea', a massive thanksgiving celebration for a bountiful harvest.
description: Pista'y Dayat (Feast of the Sea) is a grand festival held every May 1st in Pangasinan. Originally a local fishermen's thanksgiving, it has evolved into a massive provincial celebration featuring the Limgas na Pangasinan pageant, sandcastle building, and a grand parade along Lingayen Gulf.
tips: Expect massive crowds along the Lingayen Beach Boulevard.
Enjoy the numerous trade fairs and concerts.

title: Calasiao Puto Festival
shortDesc: A joyful celebration highlighting the town's famous bite-sized rice cakes.
description: The Puto Festival in Calasiao is a sweet celebration dedicated to their primary product, Puto Calasiao. The event features impressive mosaics made entirely of puto, eating contests, and vibrant street dances.
tips: Perfect time to sample new and experimental flavors of puto.
Take photos of the giant puto mosaic displays.

title: Villasis Talong Festival
shortDesc: A unique festival celebrating the eggplant, the town's leading agricultural crop.
description: Villasis is known as the vegetable bowl of Pangasinan, and the Talong (Eggplant) Festival highlights this agricultural bounty. The highlight is the street dancing where participants wear intricate costumes inspired by the purple vegetable.
tips: Watch the culinary competitions to learn new ways to cook eggplant.
Enjoy the colorful, purple-themed parade.

title: Pangasinan Provincial Capitol
shortDesc: An architectural masterpiece and the seat of the provincial government.
description: The Pangasinan Provincial Capitol Building in Lingayen is recognized as one of the most beautiful capitols in the Philippines. Constructed in 1918, this neoclassical structure features stunning columns, expansive grounds, and a rich history, standing close to the shores of the Lingayen Gulf.
tips: The building is beautifully illuminated at night.
Take a stroll around the surrounding Capitol grounds and park.

title: St. James the Great Parish
shortDesc: A centuries-old stone church built out of black coral stones.
description: St. James the Great Parish Church in Bolinao is a historic church established in 1609. What makes it unique is its construction using black coral stones. It is one of the oldest churches in the region and holds immense historical and spiritual value.
tips: Examine the unique black coral stones used in its facade.
Dress appropriately when entering.

title: Cape Bojeador Lighthouse
shortDesc: An iconic Spanish-era lighthouse offering spectacular ocean views.
description: Though located just north of Pangasinan in Ilocos Norte, this majestic Spanish-colonial lighthouse is a favorite extended road-trip destination for tourists. It has been guiding ships since 1892.
tips: Combine this visit with a trip to the wind farms.
A small museum is located at the base.

title: Sky Plaza
shortDesc: A mountain park offering sweeping views of the Caraballo Mountains.
description: Sky Plaza in Natividad is a park situated on a mountain slope. It serves as the starting point for those trekking up to the giant Christ the Redeemer statue. The plaza itself offers wonderful views, zip-lining, and picnic areas.
tips: Be prepared for a steep hike if you want to reach the Christ the Redeemer statue.
Best visited early morning for cooler temperatures.

title: Tayug Sunflower Eco Park
shortDesc: A vibrant maze of thousands of blooming sunflowers and varied flora.
description: The Tayug Sunflower Eco Park is a seasonal attraction that draws massive crowds with its thousands of towering sunflowers. The park also features vegetable picking, ornamental plant displays, and plenty of photo opportunities.
tips: Check their social media to confirm blooming season (usually early summer).
Bring an umbrella as there is little shade in the fields.
